setting-up-environment.summary: Guide to installing Python and setting up the IDE.

1.title: Installing Python
1.bodyMarkdown:
To start coding in Python, you first need to install it on your computer. You can download the latest version of Python from the official website. Here’s how to do it:

1. Go to [python.org](https://www.python.org/downloads/).
2. Click on the download link for your operating system (Windows, macOS, or Linux).
3. Run the installer and follow the instructions.

**Example:** After installation, you can check if Python is installed correctly by opening your command line and typing `python --version`. This should display the installed version of Python.

**Your turn:** Try installing Python on your computer!

2.title: Setting Up an IDE
2.bodyMarkdown:
An Integrated Development Environment (IDE) helps you write and manage your code efficiently. A popular choice for Python is PyCharm. Here’s how to set it up:

1. Download PyCharm from [jetbrains.com/pycharm](https://www.jetbrains.com/pycharm/download/).
2. Install it by following the setup instructions.
3. Open PyCharm and create a new project.

**Example:** In PyCharm, you can create a new Python file by right-clicking on your project folder and selecting 'New' > 'Python File'.

**Try it yourself:** Create a new Python file in PyCharm and write a simple print statement like `print('Hello, World!')`.
